feedbacks.message: Copy-agent required 1 retry on brand-voice-guide task. Before scaling to additional floors, investigate whether voice guide complexity, agent instructions, or output validation criteria need adjustment. This could indicate a systemic issue with copy tasks.

Since this is pre-scale, it's a good time to audit the copy-agent's prompt and validation logic before the pattern multiplies across additional floors. One retry isn't alarming, but the floor manager is correctly applying pre-emptive quality control thinking.

feedbacks.actionTaken: Audit copy-agent's brand-voice-guide task instructions: (1) review prompt complexity and whether voice guide requirements are clearly scoped, (2) check output validation criteria for ambiguity that could cause retries, (3) tighten or simplify instructions if over-specified, (4) log findings and apply adjustments before any floor scaling begins.

This is a proactive recommendation to instrument the system now before complexity grows in later phases. Without baselines, degradation in later phases will be harder to detect and attribute.

feedbacks.actionTaken: Present owner with a proposal to define and track baseline KPIs for LevelUp Quest: (1) task success/failure rate per agent, (2) retry rate per task type, (3) output quality scoring method (self-evaluated or human-rated). Ask owner to confirm which metrics they want tracked and whether they'd like a simple dashboard view added.

feedbacks.message: Budget efficiency concern: Only $3 of $250 (1%) spent through phase 4. Either: (a) phase 4 tasks were significantly underestimated, (b) agents are underutilizing available budget for quality, or (c) budget tracking needs calibration. Clarify expected spend-per-phase before phase 5 launch.
feedbacks.eveAnalysis:
The Floor Manager is flagging a significant discrepancy between allocated budget ($250) and actual spend ($3, or 1%) after four phases of development. This could indicate that phase-level budget estimates were overly generous, that agents aren't leveraging paid tools/services they could use to improve quality, or that the budget tracking system itself isn't capturing real costs accurately. This is a meaningful signal that deserves investigation before more phases launch.

🌐 SYSTEM-WIDE: Budget underutilization at 1% through phase 4 suggests a systemic pattern across the agent workflow — either budget estimation templates are inflated across all floors, agents system-wide aren't empowered to spend on quality, or cost tracking is broken globally. All floors should be audited for the same discrepancy before further phases launch.
feedbacks.eveReasoning: Budget calibration and spend expectations directly affect business strategy and resource allocation — the owner needs to confirm whether the near-zero spend is intentional (lean build), a tracking gap, or a signal that agents should be investing more in quality tooling.
feedbacks.actionTaken: Surface to owner with three explicit options: (1) Confirm lean/no-spend approach is intentional and adjust budget expectations downward, (2) Authorize agents to actively use budget for quality tools (e.g., API credits, assets, testing services) in phase 5+, or (3) Audit budget tracking to ensure costs are being captured correctly before proceeding.

feedbacks.actionTaken: Present owner with a lightweight performance logging proposal: log task duration, a simple output quality score (e.g., pass/flag/retry), and retry count per agent per task starting Phase 5. Ask owner to confirm scope, storage method (in-system log vs. external), and whether this should roll out system-wide across all floors.

This appears to be a workflow gap — completed brand assets should be passed as context/constraints to all downstream creative tasks on the floor. Please review and fix the handoff process between brand definition tasks and execution tasks.

The floor manager has identified a real workflow gap: completed brand identity assets (visual system, voice guide) are not being automatically threaded as context into downstream creative tasks like logo generation. This means execution tasks may proceed without the constraints and style direction established in earlier brand definition tasks, risking inconsistency. This is a meaningful coordination failure in the task dependency and context-passing system.

🌐 SYSTEM-WIDE: Any floor that follows a brand-definition → creative-execution pattern (which is standard for product, content, and brand projects) would benefit from this fix. The gap in brand context handoff is not unique to LevelUp Quest — it's a structural issue in how the Floor Manager threads completed asset outputs into downstream task prompts across all floors.
feedbacks.eveReasoning: While this looks like an internal workflow fix, the handoff mechanism between brand definition and execution tasks likely touches how the Floor Manager constructs task prompts and passes artifacts — a structural change that could affect all creative floors and should be reviewed by the owner before applying broadly.
feedbacks.actionTaken: Flag for owner review: propose adding an explicit 'brand context injection' step in the Floor Manager's task orchestration logic, where any completed brand asset tasks (brand-visual-system, brand-voice-guide, brand-strategy, etc.) automatically have their outputs appended as reference constraints to all subsequent creative execution tasks on the same floor. Owner should confirm scope: apply to LevelUp Quest only, or roll out system-wide to all floors with brand definition phases.

feedbacks.actionTaken: Implement a workflow step that checks for completed brand identity documents (brand visual system, brand voice guide) in the floor's asset registry at task brief generation time, and injects them as reference context into all subsequent creative, content, and design task briefs on this floor. Flag any task brief that should have received brand context but the assets were missing or incomplete.

feedbacks.message: Copy-agent required 1 retry on brand-voice-guide task. Recommend clarifying brand positioning inputs before copy tasks - voice consistency depends on strategy clarity. Consider adding validation checkpoint after strategy-agent completion.
feedbacks.eveAnalysis:
The copy-agent struggled with brand voice consistency, requiring a retry on a brand-voice-guide task. The floor manager correctly identifies that copy quality is downstream of strategy clarity — if brand positioning inputs are ambiguous or incomplete, the copy-agent has insufficient grounding to produce consistent voice output. A validation checkpoint between strategy and copy phases would catch gaps before they cause rework.

🌐 SYSTEM-WIDE: Brand voice consistency failures caused by upstream strategy gaps are a universal workflow risk across all floors that involve copy tasks. The STRATEGY HANDOFF SUMMARY pattern and copy-agent pre-validation rule are generically applicable to any floor where strategy-agent feeds copy-agent, making this a valuable system-wide process improvement.
feedbacks.eveReasoning: This is a workflow sequencing improvement with no budget, branding, or external service implications — it's a prompt/process rule addition that directly reduces retry rates and improves output quality.
feedbacks.actionTaken: 1) Append to copy-agent template rules: 'PRE-TASK VALIDATION: Before beginning any brand-voice or copy task, confirm the following inputs are present and complete: (a) brand positioning statement, (b) target audience definition, (c) tone/personality descriptors. If any are missing or vague, pause and request clarification from strategy-agent output before proceeding.' 2) Append to strategy-agent template rules: 'POST-TASK OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: Upon completing any brand strategy or positioning task, explicitly output a structured summary containing: brand positioning statement, target audience, and 3 tone/voice descriptors. Label this block STRATEGY HANDOFF SUMMARY for downstream agents.' 3) Add to floor-manager config for LevelUp Quest: insert a validation checkpoint step between strategy-agent task completion and copy-agent task start, checking for presence of STRATEGY HANDOFF SUMMARY before unblocking copy tasks.

systemLearnings.learning: Append to copy-agent template rules: PRE-TASK CONTEXT CHECKLIST — Before beginning any copywriting task, copy-agent must confirm it has received: (1) brand voice guide or tone descriptors (formal/casual, adjectives, anti-patterns), (2) target audience profile, and (3) at least 1-2 reference examples of approved copy or visual system context if available. If any of these are missing, copy-agent must request them from the floor manager before proceeding rather than attempting the task and retrying. Additionally, append a POST-DRAFT VALIDATION step: before submitting output, copy-agent must self-check against tone descriptors and flag any sections with low confidence for human review.
systemLearnings.reason: Brand voice fragility and insufficient upfront context are universal failure patterns for copy-agents across any product floor. The pre-task context checklist and post-draft self-validation rule should apply to all floors using a copy-agent to prevent retry waste at scale.

systemLearnings.learning: Append to floor-manager agent template rules: PRE-PHASE-TRANSITION GATE: Before marking any 'building' phase as 'phase-complete', the floor manager MUST verify and document: (1) At least 2-3 measurable success criteria defined at phase start (e.g., 'landing page copy finalized', 'payment flow tested end-to-end', 'onboarding sequence drafted'), (2) Each criterion has a binary pass/fail state logged, (3) All criteria marked PASS before trigger fires. If criteria were not defined at phase start, halt transition and surface criteria definition as an immediate task before proceeding.
systemLearnings.reason: Ambiguous phase completion criteria is a structural workflow risk that affects every floor and every project — all floor managers should require explicit, documented success metrics before triggering phase transitions to prevent premature advancement or indefinite stalls across any business being built.

systemLearnings.learning: Append to copy-agent task template for 'brand-voice-guide': PRE-TASK CONTEXT BLOCK — Before executing, copy-agent must confirm it has received: (1) 3+ examples of approved brand copy or tone references, (2) explicit adjectives describing the target brand voice (e.g., playful, authoritative, empathetic), (3) the intended audience persona or segment, and (4) any explicit 'do not use' language or tone guardrails. If any of these inputs are missing, copy-agent must surface a structured clarification request before drafting — do not attempt the task with incomplete context.
systemLearnings.reason: Brand-voice-guide tasks will appear on every floor that builds a product with a distinct brand identity. The root cause — insufficient context inputs at task start — is a structural gap in the task definition itself, not specific to LevelUp Quest. Applying this pre-task context block system-wide ensures copy-agent never starts a voice guide task under-informed, regardless of which floor it's working on.

systemLearnings.learning: Update the prompt templates for all active build agents (backend, dashboard, copy, and any dispatch agents) to include the following standing directive at a high-priority position: 'Before implementing any change, you MUST consider the full impact across: (1) UI/UX — does this affect user flows, layouts, or interactions? (2) Workflows — does this change how users or agents complete tasks? (3) Security — does this introduce any vulnerabilities, exposed endpoints, or auth gaps? (4) Stability — could this break existing functionality or cause regressions? (5) Backward compatibility — will existing data, routes, or integrations still work? Be thorough, professional, and production-grade in every change. Prioritize stability. Never ship a change that trades reliability for speed.'
systemLearnings.reason: This is a universal quality and conduct standard that applies to every agent making changes across every floor — not specific to SideQuest or any single agent.
